reasoning: Recent news from December 2024 states that the FDA fast-tracked a Staph aureus vaccine candidate (LBT-SA7), but it is merely entering Phase 1 clinical trials with initial results expected in the second half of 2025. Because clinical trials and regulatory approval typically take several years (requiring successful Phase 2 and Phase 3 trials), it is virtually impossible that a vaccine will be fully developed, approved, and recorded on the Wikipedia page by March 2026. Therefore, the probability is set to the absolute minimum allowed (0.02) for near-term dates. It rises slightly for distant dates to reflect slightly increased uncertainty, including the marginal chance of page vandalism persisting on a resolution date or an unprecedented emergency approval of some foreign vaccine, though both are exceptionally unlikely.
